question: 3.1.3.3.1 Locs, braids, twists, etc. styles
canonical_answer:
- All locs, braids and twists, when worn will be of uniform dimension, no wider than one inch, with natural spacing between the locs, braids, and twists and must be tightly interwoven to present a neat, professional and
well-groomed appearance.

- When worn, multiple locs, braids, or twists shall be of
uniform dimension, small in diameter (approximately ¼ inches), show no more than ¼ inch of scalp between the locs, braids, or twists and must be tightly interwoven to present a neat, professional appearance.
